keywords.name: Ninjutsu
keywords.description: Jutsu with the Ninjutsu keyword use your Ninjutsu attack bonus or Save DC when cast.

keywords.name: Genjutsu
keywords.description: Jutsu with the Genjutsu keyword use your Genjutsu attack bonus or Save DC when cast. All Genjutsu have at least one Stimuli keyword (Visual, Auditory, Inhaled, Tactile, Unaware); to be immune to a Genjutsu with multiple Stimuli, you need immunity to all of them.

keywords.name: Taijutsu
keywords.description: Jutsu with the Taijutsu keyword use your Taijutsu attack bonus or Save DC when cast.

keywords.name: Bukijutsu
keywords.description: Jutsu with the Bukijutsu keyword use your Taijutsu attack bonus or Save DC when cast.

keywords.name: Fuinjutsu
keywords.description: Jutsu with the Fuinjutsu keyword are summoning or sealing jutsu. Casting requires Chakra Seals.

keywords.name: Sensory
keywords.description: Jutsu with the Sensory keyword are designed to track and find other creatures through manipulation of chakra.

keywords.name: Clash
keywords.description: Jutsu with the Clash keyword can initiate a Jutsu Clash (see the Clashing Jutsu rules in Combat).

keywords.name: Barrier
keywords.description: Conjures, creates, or summons a defensive structure or force field that protects the caster or a designated creature. Cast as a reaction, the effect always applies to the triggering damage instance — possibly turning a hit into a miss or reducing damage. Barrier structures with their own HP intercept damage; if reduced to 0, excess damage carries over to the protected creature.

keywords.name: Stable
keywords.description: Applies only to concentration jutsu with a duration greater than instant. The caster cannot lose concentration on this jutsu as a result of taking damage.

keywords.name: Efficient
keywords.description: Applies only to concentration jutsu with a duration greater than instant. The caster does not need to spend chakra to maintain concentration.

keywords.name: Interrupt
keywords.description: Reserved for jutsu with a Reaction casting time. The Interrupt jutsu's effects apply before the triggering effect occurs, with the express purpose of preventing it entirely. Unless otherwise specified, the action and resource cost of the interrupted action is still paid.

keywords.name: Combination
keywords.description: Multiple casters can cast the jutsu simultaneously. Cost is split evenly between participants, and the effect is magnified per the Combination text. Casters who know the jutsu may join as a reaction; casters who don't but have all required keyword access within 30 ft may make a Ninshou, Martial Arts, or Illusion check vs the Save DC to assist.

keywords.name: Clone
keywords.description: Reserved for Ninjutsu. Creates duplicates controlled by the summoner, resembling the summoner's appearance. Clones use the Clone stat block (Summon Stat Blocks section) with per-jutsu tweaks. Commanded by the summoner spending an Action or Bonus Action; on command, a clone moves up to its speed and may take a Clone Action. Clones share 1 Reaction per round collectively.

keywords.name: Visual
keywords.description: Tricks the target's visual perception. Creatures with Blindness are immune to jutsu carrying only this Stimuli keyword.

keywords.name: Auditory
keywords.description: Tricks the target's perception of sound. Deafness provides immunity to jutsu carrying only this Stimuli keyword.

keywords.name: Inhaled
keywords.description: Triggered by inhaling a poison or drug. Immunity to the Envenomed condition provides immunity to jutsu carrying only this Stimuli keyword.

keywords.name: Tactile
keywords.description: Tricks the target's perception of touch, allowing the Genjutsu to deal damage. Immunity to Psychic damage provides immunity to jutsu carrying only this Stimuli keyword.

keywords.name: Unaware
keywords.description: Bypasses a creature's ability to discern real from fake. Becoming aware of an Unaware Genjutsu requires a successful Identify Genjutsu skill action. Not a Stimuli.

keywords.name: Combo
keywords.description: Until the end of your turn, you can target one affected creature with a Taijutsu Finisher regardless of range, once per turn, using either an Action or Bonus Action — ignoring its listed casting time.

keywords.name: Finisher
keywords.description: You can only use a jutsu's Finisher effect if you cast a Taijutsu with the Combo keyword immediately prior in the same turn.

keywords.name: Chain
keywords.description: See the full Chain rules in the Bukijutsu chapter for chain-rank scaling and follow-up cast effects.

keywords.name: Hijutsu
keywords.description: Unique to Clans. Jutsu with this keyword can be learned and cast ignoring ability score requirements, Medical/elemental keyword limitations, and the Kinjutsu keyword. Branch-family unique keywords are still required even with Hijutsu.

keywords.name: Kinjutsu
keywords.description: Forbidden Techniques — barred from being taught across the Five Great Nations. Cover complex techniques causing grotesque/grave bodily injury, defilement, communion with the dead, or that are too powerful for general use. Learnable only by spending downtime with a master familiar with the desired jutsu, unless granted by a class or clan feature.

keywords.name: Medical
keywords.description: Considered Medical Ninjutsu. You must have a feature or feat that allows you to add jutsu with this keyword to your jutsu list.

keywords.name: Earth Release
keywords.description: Considered Earth Release jutsu. You must have a feature or feat (e.g. the Nature Release feat) that allows you to add jutsu with this keyword to your jutsu list.

keywords.name: Wind Release
keywords.description: Considered Wind Release jutsu. You must have a feature or feat (e.g. the Nature Release feat) that allows you to add jutsu with this keyword to your jutsu list.

keywords.name: Fire Release
keywords.description: Considered Fire Release jutsu. You must have a feature or feat (e.g. the Nature Release feat) that allows you to add jutsu with this keyword to your jutsu list.

keywords.name: Water Release
keywords.description: Considered Water Release jutsu. You must have a feature or feat (e.g. the Nature Release feat) that allows you to add jutsu with this keyword to your jutsu list.

keywords.name: Lightning Release
keywords.description: Considered Lightning Release jutsu. You must have a feature or feat (e.g. the Nature Release feat) that allows you to add jutsu with this keyword to your jutsu list.

keywords.name: Unique
keywords.description: Jutsu, clan, and class features sometimes append keywords found nowhere else (such as the Hyūga clan's Branch keywords). Jutsu with these keywords can only be accessed or cast when a feature or effect explicitly grants the ability to do so.
